item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba warns of bloodbath if US attacks as Washington imposes sanctions
item.description: President Diaz-Canel warned of a bloodbath if the US attacks Cuba, while the US Treasury sanctioned Cuba's main intelligence agency and top leaders amid reports Havana obtained military drones from Russia and Iran.

item.title: 🚢 Ten countries condemn Israel's interception of Gaza humanitarian flotilla
item.description: Turkey and nine other countries issued a strong condemnation of Israel's attack on a humanitarian flotilla attempting to reach Gaza, calling it a violation of international law.

item.title: ✈️ US asks Israel to keep refueling aircraft at Ben Gurion through 2026
item.description: The US has requested Israel to keep its refueling aircraft at Ben Gurion Airport until the end of 2026, with reports indicating the presence of US aircraft is causing operational difficulties at the airport.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Fifty countries back Taiwan's WHA participation bid despite Chinese opposition
item.description: Over 50 countries have expressed support for Taiwan's participation in the World Health Assembly and other international organizations, while the WHO rejected a proposal to invite Taiwan after China vowed to block participation.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippine Senate presidency fight could shape Sara Duterte impeachment trial
item.description: The Philippine Senate convened as an impeachment court for Vice-President Sara Duterte amid a leadership battle that could influence her trial and the fallout from her father's ICC case.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇸🇦 Pakistan Deploys Fighter Jets and 8,000 Troops to Saudi Arabia Amid Iran War
item.description: Pakistan has stationed a fighter squadron and thousands of soldiers in Saudi Arabia during the Iran conflict, deepening military cooperation with Riyadh while simultaneously mediating between Tehran and Washington.

item.title: 🇬🇱🇺🇸 Greenland Says US Talks Make Progress But Island Is Not For Sale
item.description: Greenlands government reports progress in high-stakes talks with the United States over the territorys future, while firmly rejecting any possibility of sale despite President Trumps persistent interest in acquiring the strategic Arctic territory.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Russian Forces Strike Odesa and Dnipro With Missiles and Drones
item.description: Russia attacked the Ukrainian cities of Odesa and Dnipro with missiles and drones, damaging residential buildings and injuring dozens, while Ukrainian forces struck the Belgorod region, killing two people according to Russian officials.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇰🇪 France's Macron Kicks Off Kenya Visit for Africa Forward Summit
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron began a visit to Kenya ahead of the Africa Forward Summit, showcasing France's new policy for the continent - a shift from colonial power to partnership of equals amid changing global geopolitics.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Hamas Military Wing Leader Killed in Israeli Airstrike
item.description: Israel confirmed killing Izz al-Din al-Haddad, one of the last surviving architects of the October 7 attacks, in a Gaza airstrike. Hamas confirmed the death amid an ongoing fragile ceasefire.

item.title: 🇷🇺 First Russian Student Recruited From University Dies in Ukraine
item.description: Valery Averin, 23, became the first known Russian recruited directly from a university to die in Ukraine, exposing Moscow's campaign to recruit students into military service through financial incentives.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia Orders Chinese Investors to Divest From Rare Earths Company
item.description: The Australian government ordered Chinese investors to divest from Northern Minerals, a rare earths company. Analysts say the move reflects efforts to diversify supply chains away from China but risks Beijing retaliation.

item.title: 🇮🇪 Irish President's Sister Among Gaza Flotilla Activists Detained by Israel
item.description: Israeli forces intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla and detained eight Irish citizens, including the sister of Ireland's president. Dublin condemned the interception as "illegal kidnapping" of humanitarian activists.

item.title: 🇧🇴 Bolivia faces largest protests in decades as Evo Morales supporters join movement
item.description: Followers of former President Evo Morales have joined massive protests against President Rodrigo Paz, marking the biggest challenge to Bolivia's first conservative leader after nearly two decades of socialist rule. The protests are fueled by the nation's worst economic crisis in a generation.

item.title: 🇬🇱 US demands major role in Greenland through closed-door negotiations
item.description: Confidential talks between the US, Greenland, and Denmark reveal American demands for indefinite military presence and veto power over major investment deals in Greenland, aimed at countering Russian and Chinese influence in the Arctic. Greenlandic officials fear the demands would compromise their sovereignty for generations.

item.title: 🇦🇪 Drone strike targets UAE nuclear power plant amid Iran conflict tensions
item.description: A drone strike hit the Barakah nuclear power plant in the UAE, setting an electrical generator on fire. No one claimed responsibility, but suspicion fell on Iran as regional tensions remain high. Nuclear safety experts warn that nuclear facilities must not be targets in conflicts.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US-Iran Standoff Deepens as Neither Side Bends
item.description: Three months after the US-Israel attack on Iran, a US blockade and Tehran's grip on the Strait of Hormuz have created a deadlock with neither side willing to compromise, raising the risk of renewed war.

item.title: 💰 Opaque Oil Deals Around Hormuz Test the Petrodollar
item.description: The US dollar-dominated global oil trading system is being tested by the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure, as governments turn to increasingly opaque deals with Tehran and Gulf producers to secure supplies.

item.title: 🛢️ India's Cooking Fuel Shortage Driving Up California Gas Prices
item.description: A cooking gas shortage in India and $6 per gallon gasoline in California are both symptoms of the worst-ever energy supply disruption caused by the Iran war, demonstrating the global interconnectedness of fuel markets.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesian Rupiah Hits Record Low Despite Central Bank Intervention
item.description: The Indonesian rupiah slid to another record low despite central bank interventions, as global oil prices surged to two-week highs, though President Prabowo downplayed the impact on citizens.

item.title: ⚠️ Belarus and Russia Conduct Joint Nuclear Weapons Drills
item.description: Belarus launched military exercises practicing the use of Russian tactical nuclear weapons deployed on its territory, drawing strong condemnation from Ukraine.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 China Rejects Taiwan Sovereignty Claims After Leader Pledges Never to Be Sacrificed
item.description: Beijing stated Taiwan was "never a country" after Taiwanese leader William Lai Ching-te said the island would never be traded away, in the latest exchange over cross-strait sovereignty.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇹🇷🇪🇺 Germany Calls for Stronger EU-Turkey Ties, Says Ankara Key to NATO Defense
item.description: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul said Turkey should be included as the EU develops its defense policy, citing shared security interests and Turkiye role as a critical NATO ally.

item.title: 🇮🇱🏛️ Israel Opposition Demands Knesset Dissolution, Early Elections Amid Coalition Strains
item.description: Israeli opposition leaders urged lawmakers to support a bill for preliminary vote next week to dissolve parliament, as the governing coalition faces mounting internal political pressure.

item.title: ⚔️☢️ SCMP Covers Belarus Nuclear Exercises Near NATO Borders
item.description: Belarus began training exercises involving Russian tactical nuclear weapons, testing military readiness to deploy nuclear arms across different regions of the country bordering three NATO states.
